session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8947 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and follow its instructions to classify the PR feedback.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback using the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command and the git diff to understand file restructuring:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show file-level restructuring and renames in PR

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8947, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8947", "pr_title": "Refactor dangerous mode to use config-driven `live_dangerously` with `--safe` override", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5y0xyF", "path": "docs/learned/reference/cli-flag-patterns.md", "line": 88, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/diverge_fix_cmd.py:16-26` (also identical in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py:15-25`)

This entire code block (lines 79-90) copies ~10 lines verbatim from source files and will go stale if the source changes (e.g., if help text or flags are updated).

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md` (HTML comment + prose reference):
```

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 17:47:03 PT

No violations detected. All Python code changes comply with dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - No try/except for control flow detected
✅ Keyword-only arguments - 5+ parameter functions properly formatted with `*` separator
✅ No default parameter values - No violations in production code
✅ Import organization - All imports module-level and absolute
✅ Frozen dataclasses - Config fields properly defined
✅ Click CLI patterns - All commands use proper Click patterns
✅ Exception handling - Proper error boundaries maintained
✅ Path operations - Proper pathlib usage maintained

### Files Reviewed

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 17:47:15 PT

Found 0 violations across 19 files. All code adheres to dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) - None found
✅ Pathlib usage - None found
✅ Absolute imports - None found
✅ O(1) properties - None found
✅ Indentation depth - None found
✅ Exception handling - None found
✅ Re-exports - None found
✅ Parameter complexity - None found

### Code Quality Highlights
- `Ensure.resolve_dangerous()`: Clean LBYL pattern with early returns for mutually exclusive flags
- `implement.py`: Proper use of keyword-only arguments and clear flag resolution logic
- `address_cmd.py`, `diverge_fix_cmd.py`, `rebase_cmd.py`: Consistent dangerous mode resolution across all PR commands
- Test updates: Accurate comment updates reflecting new dangerous mode semantics

### Files Reviewed
- `docs/learned/cli/cli-options-validation.md`: 0 violations (documentation)

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me classify the PR feedback based on the skill instructions:
**Analysis:**
1. **PR Info**: PR #8947, "Refactor dangerous mode to use config-driven `live_dangerously` with `--safe` override"
2. **Review Threads**: - 1 unresolved review thread from `github-actions` on `docs/learned/reference/cli-flag-patterns.md:88` - The thread mentions verbatim code block copying that will go stale
3. **Discussion Comments**: - 6 informational discussion comments (plan submission, workflow started, dignified python review, test coverage review, code simplifier review, tripwires review, audit pr docs) - These are all bot-generated status updates and review summaries
4. **File Restructuring**: - The git diff shows modifications to existing files (no renames, splits, or moves visible in `--stat -M -C`) - Primary changes are to documentation and CLI commands for the dangerous mode refactoring
5. **Classification**: - The review thread from `github-actions` is actionable (bot suggestion to fix documentation pattern) - It's not pre-existing (the issue is about the new documentation added in this PR, not pre-existing code that was moved) - Complexity is **single_file** (all changes in one documentation file: `docs/learned/reference/cli-flag-patterns.md`) - The discussion comments are informational (CI status updates, review summaries)
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 8947, "pr_title": "Refactor dangerous mode to use config-driven `live_dangerously` with `--safe` override", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8947", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5y0xyF", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/reference/cli-flag-patterns.md", "line": 88, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Replace verbatim code block copy with source pointer format per docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/diverge_fix_cmd.py:16-26` (also identical in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py:15-25`)
